reasons: MV-settings-001 CONFIRMED FIXED: settings-client.tsx diff adds `disabled` prop to Toggle (native `disabled` attribute + `aria-disabled="true"` + onClick early-returns when disabled) for all 3 notification toggles (toggle-notif-approvals/apps/digest), each rendered with `disabled` set. Verified via test 'is genuinely inert via a disabled control...': fireEvent.click on all 3 leaves aria-checked unchanged. Not merely a no-op onChange as before -- the mechanism of inertness is now the real HTML disabled attribute, matching the finding's specific complaint.

Honest disclosure confirmed at settings-client.tsx (Notifications section): new

reads "Notification delivery isn't built yet — these preferences aren't functional and aren't saved by 'Save Changes' yet. Coming soon." This directly and correctly disclaims persistence.

CRITICAL check passed: grepped apps/api for 'notification' (case-insensitive) across all .py files -- zero hits are a notification-preference field or endpoint (only a billing.py comment 'hook point for a reminder notification; no state change' and an unrelated agents.py 'Notification Agent' registry entry). Confirmed `saveSettings()` in apps/web/src/lib/api/workspaces.ts only ever POSTs `profile` and `agentConfig` to /workspaces/settings -- the `save()` handler in settings-client.tsx (untouched by this diff, still lines ~227-245) never referenced notification state before or after the fix. 'Save Changes' therefore never falsely claimed to persist notification prefs it can't persist -- the claim in the commit message and disclosure text is accurate, not just newly-added spin.

MV-settings-002 CONFIRMED FIXED: 'Sync All' now calls syncAllJobBoards() -> runScoutAgent(profile.targetRole, profile.location, {token, baseUrl}) which POSTs to /agents/scout/run -- verified this is a real, pre-existing production endpoint (apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1163 @router.post('/scout/run'), backed by ScoutAgent.run() in apps/api/app/agents/scout_agent.py:51) already used identically by the Job Discovery 'Run Discovery' button (same runScoutAgent() call site in lib/api/jobs.ts:133-142) -- not a new/fake wrapper.

Fake-success and fake-spinner theater fully removed: the old `syncOne()` function (setTimeout-only local state flip, zero network calls) is deleted; the 5 per-row 'Sync' buttons (sync-greenhouse, sync-ashby, etc.) and their per-row 'Syncing...'/status-override UI are deleted. Verified via grep of the fixed source: no `syncOne`, no per-row sync-* testids remain in production code (only appear as negative assertions inside the test file confirming their absence).

Success/error notices are honest and non-stuck: on success shows role=status 'Job boards synced (checkmark)' and calls `setData(await fetchSettings())` (a real refetch, not a local flag flip); on rejection shows role=alert with the real error message and does not render 'Syncing...' anywhere afterward (button falls out of the syncing state in the finally block). Verified by reviewer swap-test that these are genuinely exercised, not tautological (see fail-before evidence below).

Gating confirmed: jobBoardSyncReady = profile.targetRole.trim() && profile.location.trim(); button is disabled and the click handler is a no-op guard (`if (!jobBoardSyncReady || jobBoardSyncing) return;`) when either field is blank/whitespace-only, preventing a guessed/garbage query from being sent to the real endpoint. Confirmed via SETTINGS_MISSING_PROFILE test fixture (targetRole:'', location:'   ') asserting syncAllBtn.disabled===true and runScoutAgentMock not called after a click.

Fail-before/pass-after independently reproduced by reviewer (not just trusted from the commit message): swapped settings-client.tsx to the fe4d5de (pre-fix) version with the new test file in place -> `pnpm vitest run notifications-jobboard.test.tsx` => 7 failed / 7 (visible failure evidence includes the old dead 'Syncing...'/per-row-button DOM and disabled===false on toggles/sync-all). Restored the fixed file (verified `git status`/`git diff` clean afterward, i.e. reviewer left no residue) -> reran -> 7 passed / 7. This rules out tautological assertions.

Full regression check: `pnpm vitest run` (whole apps/web suite) => 62 files / 429 tests passed, matching the commit message's claimed count exactly -- no regressions introduced elsewhere by the Toggle `disabled` prop addition (other Toggle call-sites at settings-client.tsx:565,572 don't pass `disabled`, remain unaffected, confirmed by green suite + clean tsc + clean build).

Scope check: diff touches exactly the two files claimed (settings-client.tsx, new test file) -- 119 lines changed in production file, no drive-by edits to unrelated code paths (career/portfolio sync at settings-client.tsx ~140-165, untouched by this diff, is a separate pre-existing REAL feature calling refreshCareerData(), not part of this fix and not fake).

Prohibited-pattern sweep of the diff: no @ts-ignore/@ts-expect-error, no `as any`, no Math.random(), no TODO/FIXME stubs, no suppressed catch blocks (both new catch blocks surface the real error message to the user via role=alert). One `eslint-disable-next-line import/first` appears in the new test file for the vi.mock-then-import pattern -- this is a PRE-EXISTING repo-wide convention (20 other occurrences across apps/web/src/**/__tests__/page.test.tsx files, e.g. app/dashboard/applications/__tests__/page.test.tsx), test-only, not a novel suppression hiding a defect; not counted as a violation.
No git-hook bypass detectable/applicable: repo has no .git/hooks/pre-commit configured, so `--no-verify` is not a live concern for this commit.

Minor non-blocking nit (does not affect verdict): STATUS_STYLE.syncing (settings-client.tsx ~line 51) is now dead code -- the old `syncing[i.name]` conditional that referenced it was removed and the backend integration `status` field values observed in apps/api (not_configured, connected, error, disconnected) never emit 'syncing', so this CSS-class map entry is unreachable. Cosmetic leftover only, no user-facing effect since it can never render; flagging for a follow-up cleanup pass, not a fix defect.
